title: Tapping for the Late Diagnosis
subtitle: A tapping practice for processing a late neurodivergent diagnosis and everything it explains
excerpt: A tapping practice for the complicated feelings that arrive with a late autism, ADHD, or other neurodivergent diagnosis. Holds both the grief of the years before the naming and the relief of finally having a map.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. The EFT tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique, developed in the mid-1990s.

practice.whenToUse: Use after receiving a late neurodivergent diagnosis, at any point in the process of making sense of it. Good for the grief of the years before the naming, and for the complexity of knowing yourself differently than you did.

content.text: A tapping practice for the complicated feelings that arrive with a late diagnosis. The relief of finally having a name for how you work. The grief of all the years before the naming. The process of understanding yourself differently, and what to do with that.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop (repeat x3)
Even though I have a new understanding of how I work, and I do not know what to do with it yet, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I am grieving the years I did not have this map, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I have complicated feelings about seeing myself in a new way, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round
**Eyebrow:** I have a new understanding of how I work.
**Side of eye:** This explains so much. And I do not know what to do with that.
**Under eye:** There is grief here. For all the years I did not know.
**Under nose:** The masking, the trying harder, the not understanding why things were harder.
**Chin:** I was not broken. I was different in a way no one named.
**Collarbone:** The diagnosis changes how I understand my past.
**Under arm:** I am allowed to grieve the years.
**Top of head:** I am also allowed to feel relief.
Reframe to positive
**Eyebrow:** This is a beginning, not just an explanation.
**Side of eye:** I know myself more clearly now.
**Under eye:** The things that were hard still exist. But now they have a name.
**Under nose:** I can build supports that fit how I actually work.
**Chin:** The diagnosis does not change who I am. It explains who I already was.
**Collarbone:** I am allowed to take my time with all of this.
**Under arm:** I do not have to have it figured out yet.
**Top of head:** I am still me. I understand myself better now.
Tap the round three times. Then tap the reframe three times.
Where this practice comes from
Original to homemade.education. EFT tapping was developed by Gary Craig in the 1990s.
